name: Screw Method (Square-1)
description: An alternative Square-1 method that takes a different approach to solving by using screw moves (repeated slash-turn sequences) as the primary mechanism. The Screw method simplifies the solving process by relying on a smaller set of intuitive move sequences. After cubeshape, the method focuses on solving corners first, then edges, using screw-like move patterns that are easy to execute and remember. It is considered a beginner-to-intermediate alternative to the Vandenbergh method.

steps.name: Cubeshape
steps.description: Restore the Square-1 to its cubic shape from any scrambled configuration. This step is the same as in other Square-1 methods — perform slash moves and layer turns to get back to a cube shape.
tips: Some shapes require more slashes than others

steps.name: Solve Corners
steps.description: Solve all eight corners using a combination of corner orientation and permutation techniques. The Screw method uses specific screw-like sequences (repeated slash patterns) to orient and place corners. Corners are solved before edges, which simplifies the process.
tips: Screw sequences are repetitive and easy to remember
Practice recognizing corner states from multiple angles

steps.name: Solve Edges
steps.description: Solve all eight edges using edge orientation and permutation algorithms. With corners already solved, edge algorithms are simpler because they only need to preserve corner positions. Handle edge parity if present.
tips: Parity may require a specific algorithm
Practice smooth slash execution for speed

pros: Simpler than Vandenbergh for many solvers
Screw patterns are intuitive and easy to remember
Good intermediate method before learning full Vandenbergh
cons: Less optimization potential than other methods
